instructions: Review and edit this file to curate the episode. Set chosen_title, adjust segment actions (keep/cut/trim), reorder arc_order, pick cold_open, approve social clips. Change status to APPROVED when done.
chosen_title: From 140GB RAM to Voice Bots: Our AI Development Journey
title_options: Building AI Monsters: Custom Rigs vs Ready-Made Solutions
Two Brooklyn Guys: AI Hardware Deep Dive & Market Reality Check
From 140GB RAM to Voice Bots: Our AI Development Journey
episode_summary: Martin and Joe dive deep into the realities of AI development, discovering that the market is already full of established players while grappling with the massive hardware requirements for serious AI work. From 140GB RAM requirements to watching AI write code for hours, they explore what it really takes to compete in the AI space.

segments.title: Opening & Recording Setup
segments.summary: Morning catch-up, introducing Fathom AI recording, and Google Meet time limits

segments.title: AI Voice Capabilities Reality Check
segments.summary: Joe's friends claim voice AI isn't ready, but Martin knows it exists and is available now

segments.title: The AI Agent Landscape Revelation
segments.summary: Discovery of existing AI sales platforms like Conversica - realization they don't need to build from scratch

segments.title: Custom PC Build Strategy
segments.summary: Planning high-performance AI development rigs, Best Buy consultation vs DIY builds

segments.title: RAM Requirements Deep Dive
segments.summary: Shocking discovery that 70B parameter models need 140GB+ RAM, questioning their approach

segments.title: Chromium Build Experience
segments.summary: Martin's mind-blowing experience building Chromium from source, AI writing thousands of lines of code

segments.title: Next Steps & Action Items
segments.summary: Dividing research tasks, Best Buy appointment details, and build timeline planning

social_clips.quote: These were the same guys that told us that voice is not possible. So they kind of lost their credibility a little bit. And then yesterday I asked him again... And then he's like, yeah, yeah, yeah. We think that's very close. It's like in the next six months to a year.
social_clips.why_compelling: Perfect example of how even AI 'experts' are behind the curve on current capabilities

social_clips.quote: So if you can see, there's one right here for sales. So something's already built out already... AiSDR, JasonAiSDR, Outreach, Sales Loft. So these are all sales agent built platform already built.
social_clips.why_compelling: Moment of realization that their idea already exists in the market

social_clips.quote: The RAM requirement, model precision. So they're saying a full precision 16 is 140-gigabyte of RAM... needed for 70 billion.
social_clips.why_compelling: Shocking technical revelation about AI hardware requirements

social_clips.quote: We live in a world now, the technology is free. It's like going to Home Depot and buying a hammer. You could go to Home Depot and buy a hammer and start building a house.
social_clips.why_compelling: Perfect metaphor for how accessible AI technology has become

social_clips.quote: I don't think it's humanly possible for somebody to do these things that it's doing in that time if you wrote the code yourself.
social_clips.why_compelling: Genuine awe at witnessing AI's coding capabilities firsthand

social_clips.quote: We almost feel like a grandfather looking at this stuff. What happened? Where did we fall off the wagon and everything just advanced to this level? It's been the last three years, bro.
social_clips.why_compelling: Relatable feeling of technological whiplash that many listeners will identify with

key_takeaways: Many AI 'experts' are still behind the curve on what's currently possible with voice AI
The AI sales agent market is already crowded with established players like Conversica and SalesLoft
Building serious AI applications requires massive hardware investments - potentially 140GB+ RAM for large models
Modern AI can write thousands of lines of code autonomously, fundamentally changing software development
The AI revolution happened incredibly fast - most powerful capabilities emerged in just the last 3-4 years

suggested_cuts.reason: Extended technical setup and casual morning chat
Brief tangent about Expo CEO change
